author: @flashlit123
text:
I may try to enter but here's an idea if anyone cares.

I already have a 3D printer so building a simple case to hold graphoil, your choice of carbon, separator, and electrolyte looks promising and should allow stacking the cells for higher voltage to light the LED.  Then you just need a charging device to have the entry.

author: @travismoore7849
text: So anyone take a stack of dead nimh or nicd cells in series of 3.6v that have lost their charge and used a D cell in series like an electron pump to run an LED?

replies.author: @travismoore7849
replies.text: Though it only works it the battery pack has reach 1v or so and will drain what little current is left. A electrostatic source may work better.

author: @WalterBurton
text: Brilliant. You're going to game The Algorithm.

author: @WalterBurton
text: Love it. Hardcore utilitarianism, in the best sense of the term. Inspiring. Transparent. Wholesome? Perhaps that last word has too much baggage. At any rate, looking forward to the entries.

author: @chuxxsss
text: Morning Rob and Luke. Rules are rules.

author: @bazofiascbr
text: I couldn't quite understand the submission process... Do I post a private video on YouTube and send you the link? Where exactly do I send it (the link or the video file)?

author: @alanblurr1265
text:
Funny enough, was on a local beach looking for some sea weed & found some strange green rocks?
Wondered whither or not can use them or some sea weed to light an LED?
If nothing else fits in on crazy enough side of an interesting to give it a go!

replies.author: @bazofiascbr
replies.text: Crazy way to light up an LED with seaweed: anaerobic digestion to produce biogas, then burn the biogas to run a Stirling engine generator, which litas an LED. As steampunk as possible! :p

author: @byrdhartley9014
text: Actually now that you have it put so succinctly, I think am going to take a swing at this. As you put the rules down I started brainstorming and getting really excited over some of my ideas. I can only imagine other potential entrants felt the exact same. I am so excited to see the entries!
